summary: Cold hydrofluoric acid is used for thoroughly removing oxide layers from base metal alloys during their preparation phase due to its effectiveness compared to alternatives which either lack precision or are less suitable under normal working conditions specified herein. The choice aligns closely with industry standards outlined concerning proper pretreatment techniques essential ahead of additional manufacturing activities targeting these types of materials accurately prepared through this method alone amongst provided options fitting those criteria best based upon available literature discussing pertinent methodologies employed across similar contexts
